IRE RESOURCE CENTER VOTER FRAUD STORIES

April 1998
Government & PoliticsInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

The IRE Resource Center maintains a library of over 12,000 investigative stories, with examples showcasing investigations into voter fraud including felons voting, duplicate votes, deceased voters, and ineligible registrations.

THE IRE RESOURCE CENTER MAINTAINS A LIBRARY OF MORE THAN 12,000 INVESTIGATIVE STORIES. OTHER EXAMPLES OF VOTER FRAUD INCLUDE: "FELONS VOTING," A 1996 WCCO-TV (MINNEAPOLIS) SERIES ON CONVICTED FELONS VOTING WHILE ON PROBATION (#13530 IN THE RESOURCE CENTER) "VOTER LISTS INVITE FRAUD," A 1995 CHICAGO SUN-TIMES LOOK INTO A SYSTEM ALLOWING DUPLICATE VOTES AND VOTES BY THE DECEASED (#I 1059) "FELONS CAST VOTES DESPITE SYSTEM," A 1995 THE HARTFORD COURANT STUDY OF CONVICTED FELONS STILL REGISTERED TO VOTE (#12342) "DEAD OR ALIVE," A 1990 ST.

LOUIS POST-DISPATCH RECORD ANALYSIS OF REGISTERED VOTERS REVEALING THOUSANDS OF INELIGIBLE VOTERS, SOME DEAD FOR YEARS (#7284) FOR COPIES OF STORIES, CONTACT THE CENTER AT (573) 882-3364 OR BY E-MAIL AT RESCNTR@IRE.ORG
